title: Removal tool for flooring planks
abstract: A decorative layer removal tool for flooring is configured with a removal shaft and either a drill attachment or handle to remove the decorative layer from a plank. The removal shaft includes a threaded end to attach to either the handle or drill attachment. The handle can be used when the user wishes to manually rotate the shaft and remove the decorative layer; the drill attachment can be used with a power drill for a motorized removal. The removal shaft includes a slit that extends from an unthreaded end to the threaded side. In typical implementations, the slit may not extend to the shaft's threads. At least a portion of the slit is used to receive the decorative surface layer from a plank, thereby enabling the decorative layer's removal.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED: 1. A removal tool configured to remove a decorative layer (620) from a plank (615), comprising: a removal shaft (105) having a fastening mechanism (110) on one end and a slit (205) that extends perpendicular to a longitudinal axis of the removal shaft (105), wherein the slit (205) extends perpendicularly from end-to-end; and a driver (115) that attaches to the removal shaftâs fastening mechanism (110), in which manipulation at the driver (115) translates to a corresponding manipulation at the removal shaft (105). 2. The removal tool of claim 1, further comprising a bevel on at least one side of the slit. 3. The removal tool of claim 1, wherein the driver is a linear shaft that, when attached to the removal shaft, is oriented substantially perpendicular to the removal shaft. 4. The removal tool of claim 3, wherein the removal shaftâs fastening mechanism is a threaded male connector, and the linear shaft includes a threaded bore that connects to the threaded male connector. 5. The removal tool of claim 4, wherein the threaded bore is substantially centered on the linear shaft. 6. The removal tool of claim 5, wherein the linear shaft is substantially cylindrical. 7. The removal tool of claim 6, wherein the threaded bore fully extends from end to end to establish a hole in the linear shaft. 8. The removal tool of claim 7, wherein a length of the threaded bore is same as a length of the threaded male connector on the removal shaft. 9. The removal tool of claim 1, wherein the driver is a drill attachment that is partially insertable into a drillâs chuck. 10. The removal tool of claim 9, wherein the drill attachment includes a bore that, when connected to the removal shaftâs fastening mechanism, is oriented parallel to the removal shaft. 11. The removal tool of claim 10, wherein the removal shaftâs fastening mechanism is a threaded male connector, and the linear shaft includes a threaded bore that connects to the threaded male connector. 12. The removal tool of claim 11, wherein on an end opposite the threaded bore is a drill bit that extends in a like direction as the bore. 13. The removal tool of claim 11, wherein a length of the threaded bore is same as a length of the threaded male connector on the removal shaft. 14. The removal tool of claim 1, wherein the fastening mechanism is one or more of a tab and notch, press-fit, threads, screw, or bolt. 15. The removal tool of claim 1, wherein the slit begins at an end opposite the fastening mechanism. 16. The removal tool of claim 15, wherein the slit extends across more than 50% of the removal shaftâs length. 17. A removal tool configured to remove a decorative layer (620) from a plank (615), comprising: a removal shaft (105) having a fastening mechanism (110) on one end and a slit (205) that extends perpendicular to a longitudinal axis of the removal shaft (105); and a driver (115) that attaches to the removal shaftâs fastening mechanism (110), in which manipulation at the driver (115) translates to a corresponding manipulation at the removal shaft (105). 18. The removal tool of claim 17, wherein the slit fully extends perpendicularly from end-to-end. 19. The removal tool of claim 17, wherein the driver is a cylindrical handle or a drill attachment, in which each driver includes a threaded bore that connects to the fastening mechanism on the removal shaft. 20. The removal tool of claim 17, wherein the slit begins at an end opposite the fastening mechanism. 15
